title: T-101 — UI: FA MRP tab (fa_mrp_tab) — schema-driven form with D365 status badges
prompt:
# T-101 — FA MRP tab UI (slice of T-025)

PRD: docs/prd/01-NPD-PRD.md §5.8 + UX fa_mrp_tab
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira
Prototype: prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/npd/fa-screens.jsx:763-804 (fa_mrp_tab)
Parent root: T-025

## Goal
Client component for FA MRP dept rendering 12 material/packaging fields (Box, Top Label, Bottom Label, Web, MRP Box/Labels/Films/Sleeves/Cartons, Tara weight, Pallet stacking plan, Box dimensions) with D365 status badges (found/nocost/missing). Uses buildDeptZod('MRP') + updateFaCell per dirty field.

## Prototype reference
**Source:** `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/npd/fa-screens.jsx:763-804`
- `cell(val, status)` helper → extract `D365StatusInput` component (Input + Badge side-by-side)
- Colors: found=#DCFCE7 (green), nocost=#FEF3C7 (amber), missing=#FEE2E2 (red); labels Found / No cost / Missing
- V04 alert at top: 'Each material validated against D365 master data. Green=Found+costed · Amber=No cost · Red=Missing'
- Refresh D365 cache button (Server Action refreshD365Cache)
- 12 fields rendered in form-grid; Save MRP + Close MRP section buttons

## Implementation contract
New `apps/web/app/(npd)/fa/[id]/_tabs/mrp-tab.tsx` 'use client'. RHF + zodResolver(buildDeptZod('MRP')). Each material field uses D365StatusInput (Input + Badge), status derived from `d365_import_cache` join (passed in via props from server component). Save calls `updateFaCell` per dirty field; Close MRP triggers deptClose modal via URL param. shadcn Form/FormField/Input/Badge/Button/Alert primitives.

## Files in scope
- `apps/web/app/(npd)/fa/[id]/_tabs/mrp-tab.tsx [create]`
- `apps/web/app/(npd)/fa/[id]/_tabs/__tests__/mrp-tab.test.tsx [create]`
- `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/npd/fa-screens.jsx [ref]`

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given the MRP tab renders, when compared to fa-screens.jsx:763-804 (fa_mrp_tab), then it shows the same 12 MRP fields in DeptColumns display_order, each material field renders D365StatusInput with Badge colored per status enum (found/nocost/missing), and uses shadcn Form/FormField/Input/Badge primitives — verified by RTL parity checklist (structural + visual + interaction).
2. Given a material has status='missing', when rendered, then the Input has red background and the Badge says 'Missing' with tooltip 'Material not in D365 — request creation'.
3. Given a user edits Tara weight and saves, when Server Action runs, then `updateFaCell` is called for `tara_weight` only and the V04 status legend stays visible.
4. UI closeout includes screenshot/artifact evidence and Playwright trace/artifacts where applicable per `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`.

## Test strategy (RED first)
- RED: 3 RTL tests covering: (a) 12 fields in correct order with correct D365 status badges, (b) missing status rendering, (c) updateFaCell call with single dirty field.
- Run: `pnpm --filter web test mrp-tab`.
- For UI parity, capture screenshots/artifacts and Playwright trace/video/artifacts where applicable; attach artifact paths in closeout.

## Risk red lines
- Do not implement other tabs in this slice
- Do not hardcode field list (use buildDeptZod + DeptColumns reference)
- Do not skip D365 status badge rendering
- Do not duplicate D365StatusInput (extract once, reuse)
